hook.text: After Krakatoa erupted in 1883, life returned to the barren island within months.

buildup.text: Spiders arrived by wind. Seeds floated in on waves. Birds followed the insects.

discovery.text: Anak Krakatau, 'Child of Krakatoa,' emerged in 1927 and now stands 150 meters tall.

twist.text: Islands destroyed by eruptions can rebuild their ecosystems faster than any other landscape.

climax.text: Life on islands is fragile but relentless—always ready to start over from nothing.

punchline.text: Destroy an island and life will come crawling back.

quiz.question: What is Anak Krakatau?
options: A new island that emerged from Krakatoa's remains
A coral atoll in the Pacific
